mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales captured by RSAFS indicate strengthening consumer demand that translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS changes are followed by negative mean 20-day SPX returns over a 3-year rolling window, or the Rank IC between RSAFS changes and 20-day returns is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals a weakening labor market that reduces household income and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE has risen > 0.3pp over 3 months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: High FEDFUNDS combined with declining INDPRO creates a double drag on equity valuations through elevated discount rates and falling earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: A linear interaction term between FEDFUNDS and INDPRO changes fails to achieve a t-statistic < -2.0 for predicting 60-day returns, or the strategy Sharpe ratio is below 0.2 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, accelerating consumer price inflation captured by CPIAUCSL forces the market to discount future earnings at higher real rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In identified late-cycle expansions, months with positive CPIAUCSL momentum fail to produce negative mean 20-day SPX returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contraction in industrial production directly signals reduced corporate output and revenue compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative month-over-month INDPRO changes are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,800 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and downstream durable goods demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Whenever HOUST is below 1800, positive 60-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50% of months over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising unemployment and a declining federal funds rate identifies a recessionary easing environment where equity risk premiums spike, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings revisions lag policy.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over 2 months AND FEDFUNDS declines > 0.25pp over 2 months
failure_condition: The joint condition triggers positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term in a bivariate regression has a positive coefficient (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP captured by GDPC1 reflects aggregate demand expansion that lifts broad corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 surprises fail to produce positive mean 60-day SPX returns (t-test p > 0.05) over any 5-year rolling window.
